description: Pre-Session B classification for soul (reg 182). Sets anchor verse (Gen 2:7). Extracts all None-status psuchē sub-glosses and psuchē-compound terms. Sets H5317 no.phet as phase2_enrichment per prior Session B decision. Corrects null anchor_verse on registry record.

operations.description: Set anchor verse for soul registry. Gen 2:7 — the constitutive verse: the moment when nephesh comes into existence. God breathes the breath of life into dust and man becomes a living soul (nephesh cha.yah). No other verse in the corpus describes the origin of the soul. This is the irreplaceable structural anchor for the entire registry.

set.status_note: Primary Greek soul term. 46 verses. Covers full range: soul vs body (Mat 10:28), soul as life-force (Joh 12:25), soul as the person (1Pe 2:11), eschatological soul (Rev 6:9), the soul of Christ (Mat 26:38). Most analytically important Greek term in registry.
Soul-as-life sub-gloss. 33 verses. The 'lay down one's life' cluster (Joh 10:11,15,17; 15:13) and 'save your life/lose it' paradox (Mat 16:25 etc). Christologically dense.
Reflexive soul use. 6 verses including Magnificat (Luk 1:46 — 'My soul magnifies the Lord'), Gethsemane (Mar 14:34), and Joh 12:27 ('Now is my soul troubled').
Collective soul/person use. 8 verses including Act 2:41 (3000 souls added), Rom 13:1 (every person subject to authorities).
Animal soul sub-gloss. 0 verses in export. Structurally important: same term covers both human soul and animal life — parallels the Hebrew nephesh.

The soul-vs-spirit boundary term. 5 verses: 1Co 2:14 (natural person does not accept Spirit's things), 1Co 15:44 (sown natural body, raised spiritual body), Jam 3:15, Jude 19. Analytically critical for the spirit-soul distinction.
Soul departing at death. 3 verses: Act 5:5 (Ananias), Act 5:10 (Sapphira), Act 12:23 (Herod). Death as the ekpsuchē — the soul going out.
Interior soul-division. 2 verses: Jam 1:8, Jam 4:8. The double-minded person is di-psychos — soul divided between God and world. The Being-dimension disorder of the soul.
Soul fainting. 1 verse: Luk 21:26 — people fainting with fear at eschatological upheaval. Soul-capacity failing under overwhelming circumstances.
Without soul/lifeless. 1 verse: 1Co 14:7 — lifeless instruments. Defines soul by its absence — what makes a living being different from an instrument.
Soul-gladness. 1 verse: Phi 2:19 — 'that I too may be cheered (eu-psycheō).' Soul-wellbeing vocabulary.
Like-souled/like-minded. 1 verse: Phi 2:20 — Timothy genuinely concerned (iso-psychos). Soul as the seat of concern and orientation.
Small-souled/fainthearted. 1 verse: 1Th 5:14 — 'encourage the fainthearted (oligopsuchos).' Soul-diminishment vocabulary — the contraction of the inner person under pressure.
Same-souled/harmonious. 1 verse: Phi 2:2 — 'being in full accord (sumpsuchos).' Community identity through shared soul-orientation.

Soul refreshed/restored. 3 verses: Exo 23:12 and 31:17 (even God rested and was refreshed on the seventh day — the only verse in Scripture attributing soul-refreshment to God). 2Sa 16:14 (David refreshed). Connects soul to Sabbath rest and divine life.

operations.description: H5317 no.phet [honey] — phase2_enrichment. Carry forward decision from prior Session B work. Pro 27:7 ('one who is full loathes honey, but to one who is hungry everything bitter is sweet') encodes the appetite dimension of nephesh through the honey-as-archetype-of-desire metaphor. Not a primary soul term but enriches the appetite sub-dimension. PH2 flag to be raised in Session B analysis.

set.status_note: Pro 27:7 encodes the appetite dimension of nephesh through honey-as-archetype metaphor. Not primary soul vocabulary. Decision carried forward from prior Session B.

notes: All psuchē sub-glosses now extracted. G5591 psuchikos extracted — critical for spirit-soul boundary analysis. H5314 na.phash extracted — contains the only verse attributing soul-refreshment to God (Exo 31:17).
